title: buildah-bud tests: handle go pseudoversions, plus...
body:
Handle go pseudoversions, e.g. a custom non-released buildah
used during testing of a PR. This will be something like:

   v1.20.1-0.20210402144408-36a37402d0c8

...and it makes it impossible (AFAIK) to do a shallow checkout;
we need to do a full clone of buildah, then git-checkout the
SHA (last element of the long string above).

FIXME: this is great for testing, but we almost certainly
want some way to block this PR from merging, don't we?

And, while testing this, found and fixed two bugs:

  - quote "$failhint" when echoing it on failure; otherwise
    we lose original whitespace.

  - invoke git-am with --reject! This makes it SO MUCH EASIER
    to identify the failing part of our patch!

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>

The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [edsantiago] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Oh, it'll pass (correction: has passed). The test will be on a PR like your #9896 which brings in a non-tagged buildah. (I tested it on my end, of course.)
I added this patch to my PR with the vendored version of Podman, if the tests pass, we can merge both.
